title: glaucium corniculatum var. tricolor
intro: Glaucium corniculatum var. tricolor, commonly known as the Red Poppy or Corn Poppy, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Papaveraceae family. It is a perennial plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers have been used to treat various health conditions, such as coughs, colds, and respiratory issues, thanks to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote expectoration. Glaucium corniculatum var. tricolor can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
